blockingIssues.description: Commit 1269490 only changed the Playwright regression to click Calendar after returning to `/timer`; the runtime still restores persisted TimerView state from local storage, so the requested calendar-by-default return behavior remains unimplemented.

The feature claims commit `568e34d` as the auth-navigation repair, but that diff only edits `apps/website/e2e/timer-page.spec.ts` and does not provide a scoped implementation unit for the editor auth flow.

The reviewed handoff points to commit `7835272`, which does not contain the settings success-toast fix; the actual test assertion repair landed earlier in commit `266727a`, so the feature is not traceable to a commit-local implementation.

The feature attributes the Stop timer stabilization to refactor commit `45e0674`, but the actual Playwright/runtime fix lives in earlier commit `94f7496`, leaving this feature without valid scoped implementation evidence.

Commit `94f7496` includes the intended `pollSessionBootstrap` stabilization, but it also rewrites `VAL-CROSS-005` into a broader running-timer continuity scenario outside the assigned flake-fix scope.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Tighten `frontend-test-worker` completion rules so handoff `commitId` must directly contain the scoped fix or explicitly state that the work was already satisfied by an earlier commit instead of citing an unrelated green rerun.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Four reviews in this round failed because the submitted handoff commit (`568e34d`, `7835272`, `45e0674`) did not contain the claimed fix even though the repository was green.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require flaky-fix and regression-fix features to stay narrowly scoped; if a stabilization commit also rewrites unrelated assertions or expands behavior coverage, the worker should split that work into a separate feature or explain the coupling before handoff.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `tracking-ui-read-session-bootstrap-flake-fix` review found the intended bootstrap polling fix bundled with an unrelated `VAL-CROSS-005` test rewrite, and the calendar-return fix masked behavior drift by changing the test instead of the runtime.

rejectedObservations.observation: Apply a shared-state update documenting that the settings success-toast fix lives in commit `266727a` and the Stop timer stabilization lives in `94f7496`.
rejectedObservations.reason: rejected because commit-location breadcrumbs are transient review evidence rather than durable worker guidance; the real issue is inaccurate feature handoff traceability, which belongs in skill guidance.

rejectedObservations.observation: Escalate missing `agent-browser` narration as a blocking defect for all reviewed frontend-test-worker features.
rejectedObservations.reason: rejected because the blocking problems in this round were masked behavior changes, scope drift, and incorrect commit attribution; lack of browser narration alone was not sufficient to change pass/fail outcomes.
